metadata.name: Hexaëder #141
metadata.description:
Hexaëder (cube) is a research into abstraction and a search for a balance between simplification and visual complexity.

No matter how simple and unambiguous things appear to be, in reality they are often more complex. Enjoy the simple, explore the complex.

Hexaeder has a wide variety of possible outcomes. I have put together 10 different color palettes; the number of cubes ranges from 10 to 401 (18 different grids); each cell can have 4 different background patterns; each cube can be filled in 4 distinct ways; there are 5 line colors and 16 different "cube figures"; the vignette can be black or be the color of the palette, and there are several "texture settings". This results in over 1.8 million possibilities.

For more information see: https://www.behance.net/gallery/131114761/Hexaeder-Flattened-cubes

metadata.name: Breeding Grounds #55
metadata.description:
Social Networks.
Molecular Clouds.
Neuronal Circuitries.
Quantum Mechanical Structures.

Blurring Boundaries.
Infinite Self-Similarities.

Total.
Mess.

Perfect.
Order.

/////
Each element (besides the painting ground textures) is based on the underlying subdivided grid which is sometimes exposed like some kind of preliminary drawing.

Note on the split:
After seeing some of the outputs, Sermin had a dream about “Breeding Grounds” and I decided to incorporate events and feelings from her dream into the project. That's how the names of the color palettes came about. Thank you for naming them, Sermin!

Press 's' to save a .png with the current resolution (2000x3000).
If you want a file with higher resolution and/or without the “Painting Ground Textures” for printing please contact me via Twitter or Discord. 

/////
Created with p5.js

Twitter: @serminmin / @pxlshrd

metadata.name: Bits Balanced #199
metadata.description:
Mathematically balanced structures inspired by pixel art.

Features:
1 - Toggle Border
S - Save

Query Parameters:
grain=false - turns off grain texture
resolution=2048 - sets resolution
frame=false - turns frame off

Credit:
Thanks to @Estienne_ca for the grainy texture algorithm

metadata.name: Balanced #110
metadata.description:
What does balance look like when viewed through the lens of pure mathematics? This is the question Balanced attempts to answer.

Each output is composed of a unique collection of blocks mathematically stacked to be in perfect balance.

This is my Genesis project. Enjoy!

Interactions:
1 - Reverse your stack vertically and re-balance.
2 - Toggle between square and portrait frame.
3 - Show center of mass. 
4 - Toggle the border.
5 - Toggle the background (color/transparent)
6 - Export image (2048x2048)
7 - Export image (4096x4096)
8 - Export image (8192x8192)

How it was made: https://www.jeffweber.art/balanced
